zh.Buckenham Castle, Buckenham, Norfolk, En: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可巴肯哈姆
zh.Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可阿申維爾索普
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Thorpe於大約1398年出生在英國諾福爾可阿申維爾索普時，她的父親Edmund Thorpe57歲，母親Joan de Northwode41歲。她於1431年1月6日之前與John Clifton在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1477年12月1日在英國素福爾可賀瑟特享年80歲。

zh-hans.Buckenham Castle, Buckenham, Norfolk, En: 英国英格兰諾福爾可巴肯哈姆
zh-hans.Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可阿申維爾索普
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Thorpe于大约1398年出生在英国諾福爾可阿申維爾索普时，她的父亲Edmund Thorpe57岁，母亲Joan de Northwode41岁。她于1431年1月6日之前与John Clifton在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世于1477年12月1日在英国素福爾可賀瑟特享年80岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Thorpe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): from Middle English thorp(e) ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English thorp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from any of several places called Thorp(e), which are most frequent in Yorkshire, the Midlands, and East Anglia. Compare Throop .

variants.name: Trupp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Hermann, Reinhold.

English (Herefordshire and Monmouthshire): variant of   Thorpe  , or from a place so named, such as Thrupp (Berkshire, Gloucestershire, Oxfordshire), Thrupe (Somerset), Throop (Dorset, Hampshire, Somerset), and Throope (Wiltshire).

variants.name: Throop
meanings: English: from Middle English throp ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English throp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from Throop in Hampshire, Throope in Wiltshire, Thrup in Oxfordshire, or any of the places called Thrupp in Berkshire, Gloucestershire, and Northamptonshire. Compare Thorpe .

variants.name: Tharpe
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Thorpe  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Thorp
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Thorpe  .

variants.name: Troup
meanings: Scottish (eastern): habitational name from a place in the parish of Gamrie, near Banff. The placename is unexplained, perhaps a Pictish cognate of Welsh trwp ‘vessel, dish, basin’.
Possibly also English, a metathesized form of   Thorpe  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Thorpe was born about 1398, in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, her father, Edmund Thorpe, was 57 and her mother, Joan de Northwode, was 41. She married John Clifton before 6 January 1431, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died on 1 December 1477, in Hessett, Suffolk, England, at the age of 80.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Thorpe est née environ 1398, à Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Thorpe, avait 57 ans et sa mère, Joan de Northwode, avait 41 ans. Elle a épousé John Clifton avant 6 janvier 1431, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 décembre 1477, à Hessett, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 80 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Thorpe um 1398 in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edmund Thorpe 57 und ihre Mutter Joan de Northwode 41. Sie hat vor 6. Januar 1431 in England, United Kingdom John Clifton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. Dezember 1477 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Hessett, Suffolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Thorpe è nata il circa 1398 nel Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, sua padre, Edmund Thorpe, aveva 57 anni e sua madre, Joan de Northwode, aveva 41 anni. Lei sposato John Clifton prima del 6 gennaio 1431, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 1 dicembre 1477, nel Hessett, Suffolk, England, all'età di 80 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Thorpeが約1398年にイギリスノーフォルクアシュウエルソルペで生まれた時，父Edmund Thorpeは57歳，母Joan de Northwodeは41歳であった。1431年1月6日以前にイギリスイギリスでJohn Cliftonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1477年12月1日にイギリススフォルクヘセットで80歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Thorpe(이)가 대략 1398년 영국 노르폴크 애스h웰소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Thorpe(은)는 57세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Northwode(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 1431년 1월 6일 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 John Clifton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1477년 12월 1일 영국 수폴크 헤싯에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Thorpe nasceu aproximadamente 1398, em Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund Thorpe, tinha 57 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Northwode, tinha 41 anos. Ela casou-se com John Clifton antes 6 de janeiro de 1431, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de dezembro de 1477, em Hessett, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 80 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Thorpe родилась около 1398 г. в местности ашвеллтаурп, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund Thorpe было 57 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Northwode, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Clifton перед 6 январь 1431 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла 1 декабря 1477 г. в местности хессетт, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 80 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Thorpe nació aproximadamente 1398, en Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Edmund Thorpe, tenía 57 años y su mamá, Joan de Northwode, tenía 41 años. Se casó con John Clifton antes de 6 de enero de 1431, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 1 de diciembre de 1477, en Hessett, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 80 años.
